bibliographic_data.title: The Metallicity Distribution in the Halo Stars of NGC 5128: Implications for Galaxy Formation
bibliographic_data.abstract: We have used the Hubble Space Telescope to obtain WFPC2 (V, I) photometry for stars in the halo of NGC 5128, the nearest giant elliptical galaxy. The resulting color-magnitude diagram (CMD) of this field, which lies ∼21 kpc from the center of the galaxy, contains more than 10,000 stars and reaches almost 3 magnitudes down the red-giant branch (RGB). From the sharply defined RGB tip at I = 24.1 ± 0.1 and MI(tip) = −4.1, we obtain a distance to NGC 5128 of 3.9 Mpc. Comparison with the fiducial RGBs of Milky Way globular clusters and model isochrones demonstrates that this outer-halo population of NGC 5128 is completely dominated by old stars, with an extremely broad metallicity range extending from the most metal-poor Galactic globulars at [Fe/H] < ∼−2 up to above-solar abundance. The relative contribution from any younger, bright asymptotic-branch component is negligible.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study provides a detailed CMD of the outer halo stars in NGC 5128, revealing an extremely broad metallicity range and supporting a two-component model of closed-box chemical enrichment.
core_content.key_methodology: Hubble Space Telescope WFPC2 (V, I) photometry was used to obtain a color-magnitude diagram for more than 10,000 stars in the outer halo of NGC 5128. The data were analyzed using DAOPHOT II and transformed into V, I magnitudes.
outcomes: The metallicity distribution function (MDF) can be well matched by a simple two-component model of closed-box chemical enrichment.
Two-thirds of the stars belong to the metal-richer component with Z0 ≃ 0.18Z⊙, and one-third to the metal-poorer component with an initial abundance Z0 = 0.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study uses Hubble Space Telescope WFPC2 (V, I) photometry to analyze the outer halo of NGC 5128. The resulting CMD contains over 10,000 stars and reaches almost three magnitudes down the red-giant branch. From this data, a distance to NGC 5128 is calculated at 3.9 Mpc. The metallicity distribution function (MDF) of the outer halo population shows an extremely broad range from very metal-poor to above-solar abundance, supporting a two-component model of closed-box chemical enrichment.

nodes.label: Two-Component Model of Closed-Box Chemical Enrichment
